chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Cr. Bail Appl. No. S- 742 of 2025 [ Qasim Shah v. The State ] M/s. Mian Taj Muhammad Keerio & Peeral Majeedano, Advocates for Applicant Complainant present in person, his counsel Mr. Dilshad Gahoti is called absent. Mr. Altaf Hussain Khokhar, A.P.G. Date of Order: 4.2.2026 O R D E R OMAR SIAL, J. - Qasim Shah seeks post-arrest bail in crime number 18 of 2025, registered under sections 324, 506/2, 147, 148, and 149 P.P.C. at the Khyberani police station. The F.I.R. was registered on 30.04.2025 on the complaint of Aijaz, who narrated an incident that occurred on 17.03.2025. Aijaz recorded that he and two others were going on a motorcycle when they were intercepted by Qasim Shah (armed with a rifle), Saleem Khan (armed with a rifle), and Naeem Shah (armed with a double-barreled shotgun) and Gulzar (armed with a pistol) and Mohammad Umar (armed with a rifle). Umar fired at and hit Aijaz on his chest whereas the fire made by Qasim Shah lacerated the area above his right thumb. The assailants then went away. 2. I have heard the applicant’s counsel and the Assistant Prosecutor General. 3. The shot attributed to the applicant injured the hand of the complainant. The punishment for the injury inflicted by him would either be bailable or fall within the non-prohibitory clause of section 497 P.P.C. Keeping in mind the principles enunciated in Tariq Bashir and 5 others vs The State (PLD 1995 SC 34), I do not see any exceptional or extraordinary reason to deny the applicant bail. 4. Above are the reasons for the short order dated 04.02.2026. JUDGE karar_hussain/PS*
